description: Confounder: The model assumes any t plugged into min_snr_5_weight behaves like the SNR=5 cas

_meta.explanation: The rule 'In RectifiedFlowLoss with weighting='min_snr_5', the loss weight reaches its maximum value of 5/6 when the SNR equals 5, which occurs at time t = 1/(1+√5).' does NOT hold here. The model assumes any t plugged into min_snr_5_weight behaves like the SNR=5 case, but cosine_sample_median() returns t = 1 - cos(π/4) ≈ 0.2929, which yields SNR ≈ 11.66 > 5, activating the min(snr, 5.0) clamp. This clamped SNR=5 then produces weight = 5/(5+1) = 5/6, but only if the SNR were exactly 5; however, the weight formula uses the original unclamped SNR in the denominator, not the clamped one — a subtle implementation detail.
_meta.curriculum_note: Rule 'In RectifiedFlowLoss with weighting='min_snr_5', the loss weight reaches its maximum value of 5/6 when the SNR equals 5, which occurs at time t = 1/(1+√5).' holds in the obvious case but breaks here. Distractor B is the hard negative — the rule naively applied.
